title: Mauritius becomes 70th nation to sign the Artemis Accords
summary: Mauritius signed the Artemis Accords on July 17, becoming the 70th nation to join the international agreement in under six years. The Artemis Accords aim to establish guidelines for responsible and sustainable lunar exploration.

title: Space Force triples launch contract ceiling amid rising demand
summary: The U.S. Space Force increased the maximum value of the National Security Space Launch Phase 3 Lane 1 contract from $5.6 billion to $17 billion to meet rising demand. This move reflects the growing need for secure space launch services.

title: Pentagon awards $7.1 million to expand U.S. production of cover glass for satellites
summary: The Pentagon awarded $7.1 million to Martin Materials Solutions to expand U.S. production of radiation-resistant cover glass for satellites. The funding, under the Defense Production Act, supports spacecraft solar arrays and thermal-control systems.

title: Skyroot Aerospace reaches orbit on first Vikram-1 launch
summary: Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket successfully reached orbit on its inaugural flight on July 18, marking the first commercial Indian rocket to achieve this milestone.

title: NASA terminates Draper lunar lander mission
summary: NASA has terminated a lunar lander mission in collaboration with Draper due to significant delays in spacecraft development. The decision marks the end of plans for the previously scheduled mission.

title: SpaceX’s only problem is finding more space to work with
summary: SpaceX's IPO has made it a mainstream capital markets story, highlighting its vision for interplanetary civilization. The company's primary challenge now is securing more launch and operational space.

title: Serbia signs the Artemis Accords
summary: Serbia has signed the Artemis Accords, aligning with the U.S.-led lunar exploration initiative. This move comes over two years after Serbia joined China's competing lunar base project.

title: SpaceX aborts Starship Flight 13 launch attempt
summary: SpaceX aborted the launch of its Starship Flight 13 on July 16 due to some engines failing to ignite. This marks another delay in the vehicle's testing and development process.

title: SpaceX launches 21 satellites for military data network
summary: SpaceX launched 21 satellites for the Space Development Agency's military data network. The mission completes half of the planned Tranche 1 Transport Layer constellation.

title: Nature study identifies a new way to spot nuclear weapons in orbit
summary: A study in Nature Astronomy proposes a new satellite design to detect thermonuclear warheads in orbit. The research is partially funded by unspecified sources.

title: 'Aliens' at 40: James Cameron's sequel is a sci-fi icon, but did you know he quit the movie twice?
summary: James Cameron's 'Aliens' sequel faced studio issues that led him to quit twice before its release 40 years ago. The film remains a sci-fi icon despite early production challenges.

title: Should we trust AI in the search for alien life? Scientists aren't so sure
summary: New research indicates that AI systems can be repeatedly tricked into incorrectly identifying non-life forms as life, raising doubts about their reliability in the search for alien life. Scientists emphasize the need for caution when relying on AI for such critical missions.

title: How Christopher Nolan's 'Interstellar' turned general relativity into the bad guy
summary: Christopher Nolan's 'Interstellar' is highlighted for its portrayal of time dilation as a central theme in the film. The movie is revisited as Nolan begins work on 'The Odyssey'.

title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers how black holes feed themselves
summary: Astronomers have used the James Webb Space Telescope to observe how supermassive black holes feed themselves. The discovery provides new insights into the mechanisms behind black hole growth.

title: Scientists told them, 'No, it's too dangerous,' but they did it anyway: Inside Japan's super-close asteroid flyby
summary: Japan's Hayabusa2 spacecraft performed a daring super-close flyby of a near-Earth asteroid on July 5, capturing spectacular images despite initial warnings of danger. The mission required heated debate before teams approved the risky maneuver.

title: Another reusable rocket? Japan launches, lands RV-X  prototype (video)
summary: Japan successfully conducted the first flight test of its RV-X experimental reusable rocket prototype. The test included both launch and landing of the vehicle.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 219 — SpaceX Goes to War!
summary: The latest episode of the 'This Week In Space' podcast discusses recent space headlines, with a focus on SpaceX's activities. The episode covers key developments in the space industry over the past few weeks.

title: 'We knew we had a lot to live up to': As 'Aliens' turns 40, we chat with the legendary VFX masters who created the Alien Queen (interview)
summary: An interview with VFX artists Shane Mahan and Lindsay MacGowan from Legacy Effects, who worked on the iconic Alien Queen from the 1986 film 'Aliens', reflects on the challenges and legacy of their work 40 years later.

title: Beyond the Perseids: 6 meteor showers worth watching this summer
summary: The Perseids meteor shower is the most famous, but six other lesser-known meteor showers will also be visible this summer, offering additional celestial events to watch.

title: NASA Pushes New Wing Design to Find Structural Limits
summary: NASA tested a new lightweight, long, and thin wing design called SWEET-15 to evaluate its structural limits. The experiment showed promising results even when pushed beyond intended boundaries.

title: The Growing Crescent of Mars as NASA’s Psyche Mission Approaches
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured a composite image of Mars' crescent growing as the spacecraft approached for a gravity assist in May 2026. The images show Mars' crescent expanding as the spacecraft neared the planet.

title: Establishing a VTE Risk Score for Astronauts Algorithm
summary: NASA's Office of the Chief Health and Medical Officer (OCHMO) initiated a working group in April 2026 to review updated VTE case information and discuss progress on mitigating VTE risk during spaceflight. The group analyzed altered blood flow status in astronauts to develop a VTE Risk Score algorithm.

title: NASA Awards Facilities Support Services Contract for Ames Research Center
summary: NASA has awarded a contract to Chugach Intelligence Solutions LLC for facilities support services at Ames Research Center. The five-year hybrid contract will ensure the center's facilities remain operational and support NASA's missions.

title: NASA’s Chandra and IXPE Study Pulsar in Lighthouse Nebula
summary: NASA's Chandra and IXPE telescopes have captured a composite image of a pulsar within the Lighthouse Nebula, combining X-ray data to study its properties. The image, released in July 2026, highlights the pulsar's strong magnetic field and rapid spin.

title: La NASA invita a los medios de comunicación al lanzamiento del telescopio espacial Roman
summary: NASA has opened media accreditation for the launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, scheduled for no earlier than 7:20 a.m. EDT on August 30. The telescope's launch is part of NASA's ongoing space science missions.

title: NASA Invites Media to Roman Space Telescope, Crew-13 Launches
summary: NASA has opened media accreditation for the upcoming launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope and the SpaceX Crew-13 mission, both scheduled for the coming months. The Roman telescope is set to launch no earlier than 7:20 a.m. EDT on August 30 aboard a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Images Details of Martian Surface During Flyby
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured enhanced-color images of Mars' surface during a flyby on May 15, 2026, using its multispectral imager instrument.

title: NASA Welcomes Mauritius as 70th Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: The Republic of Mauritius has joined the Artemis Accords as the 70th signatory, becoming the seventh African country to commit to responsible space exploration. NASA's Deputy Administrator Matt Anderson welcomed Mauritius during a signing ceremony in Ébène.

title: A Tide-Fueled Trove of Biodiversity in Guinea-Bissau
summary: The Bijagós archipelago in Guinea-Bissau features diverse ecosystems like mudflats, sandy beaches, and mangrove forests that sustain migratory shorebirds and sea turtles. This biodiversity highlights the ecological importance of the region.

title: Starship Flight 13: Booster 20 Aborts During Ignition
summary: Starship Flight 13 was aborted during ignition due to Booster 20 failing to ignite properly. The launch attempt on July 16 at 5:45 pm CDT was halted as a result.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is aiming for its first land recovery.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: The Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome on July 14. This marks another mission supporting ongoing operations aboard the ISS.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS), while SpaceX's Starship is scheduled for its 13th flight test. Seven launches are planned globally this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key milestone ahead of its planned launch.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: SpaceX completed a record-duration static fire test of Booster 20's 33 engines. This marks a significant milestone in the company's development efforts.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays. The company reported significant gains in Electron launches during this period.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: New launch pads are being constructed at Cape Canaveral for Stoke Space and Relativity Space as part of the Space Coast's ongoing revitalization. This development highlights the growing commercial space industry activity in the region.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station aims to serve as a private orbital outpost.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Test firing at brand new green chemical propulsion lab
summary: A test firing was conducted at a newly established green chemical propulsion laboratory. This marks a significant step in developing environmentally friendly propulsion technologies for space missions.

title: The art of hadrons
summary: The news headline 'The art of hadrons' suggests a creative or artistic interpretation of particle physics, likely related to CERN or particle accelerator research. No specific company or detailed context is provided in the snippet.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025, aiming to inform industry about planned activities and opportunities. These events will facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The news discusses a significant advancement in robotic arm technology for space applications. It highlights the potential of a single robotic arm to perform multiple critical tasks in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on June 2nd at its technical center in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and ensuring the durability of materials for spacecraft assembly. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section focuses on developing materials capable of withstanding the harsh conditions of space.

title: Beyond Reach Labs Raises $10M Seed, Moves to NYC
summary: Beyond Reach Labs secured a $10 million seed funding round and relocated its headquarters to New York City. The company focuses on addressing size, weight, and power constraints in spaceflight.

title: NASA Selects Starlink for Artemis III Mission
summary: NASA has awarded SpaceX a contract to provide laser communications capabilities for the Artemis III mission using Starlink. This partnership highlights the growing role of commercial space technologies in NASA's lunar exploration plans.

title: ISU Founder Shares a Vision Beyond Liquidation
summary: ISU cofounder Bob Richards and Dean Emerita Angie Bukley discussed the aftermath of ISU's liquidation and their vision for the future. The interview explores what led to the liquidation and potential next steps for the organization.

title: SWISSto12 Closes $70M Series C
summary: SWISSto12 has raised $70 million in a Series C funding round to scale production for sovereign space hardware. The European space company aims to meet global demand for in-orbit solutions.

title: TraCSS Looms Large Over Mission Authorization Hearing
summary: A hearing reviewed the Office of Space Commerce's proposed mission authorization framework, questioning its financial feasibility. The TraCSS system was a major topic of discussion during the hearing.

title: Falcon 9 Sales Freeze Anxiety and Our Launch Ramp Forecast Model for New Rockets
summary: The article discusses concerns over a potential sales freeze of SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket, which could disrupt the space industry's reliance on reliable launch services. It explores the impact on satellite businesses and the future of new rocket launches.

title: Exclusive: Loft Orbital Scales Offering with Apex Bus Purchase
summary: Loft Orbital acquired new satellite bus inventory to support larger, high-power customer payloads. The purchase aims to expand their offerings in the space industry.

title: NatSec Space Policy Nominees Face Questions from Senators
summary: Pentagon's nominee for space acquisition testified that legacy military space programs are incompatible with modern acquisition reforms. Senators questioned nominees on space policy during the hearing.

title: Payload Field Guide: NSSL
summary: The U.S. Space Force expanded its National Security Space Launch (NSSL) program by adding two new launch providers on July 8, bringing the total number of companies under the program to seven.

title: Spinifex Space Launches With Australian Test Ranges
summary: Spinifex Space has launched operations to offer end-to-end suborbital launch campaigns and private range access in Australia. The company will also provide test and evaluation infrastructure.

title: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace Partner for Lunar Exploration Ambitions
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace have formed a partnership through a new Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) focused on lunar exploration. The collaboration involves exchanging technical information and conducting system compatibility studies.

title: HawkEye 360 Details Tactical Direct Downlink in Valiant Shield Exercise
summary: HawkEye 360 demonstrated a tactical direct downlink during the Valiant Shield military exercise, showcasing a machine-to-machine pipeline in collaboration with Lockheed Martin.

title: Starlink Laser Communications Will Support NASA Artemis III
summary: NASA's Artemis III mission will utilize SpaceX's Starlink mini laser terminals for downlinking imagery and video to mission control in Houston. The agreement was announced on Thursday.

title: BQP Secures SpaceWERX Funding for Quantum-Assisted AI for Space Domain Awareness
summary: BQP secured SpaceWERX funding to develop a quantum-assisted AI platform for space domain awareness (SDA). This marks the company's first federal contract.

title: Space Force Chief Nominee Schiess Counts Moving Target Indication Among Top Priorities
summary: U.S. Space Force Lt. Gen. Douglas Schiess prioritizes developing space-based moving target indication systems and disrupting Chinese military space systems as key deterrence measures. His nomination highlights the growing focus on space-based surveillance and counterspace capabilities.

title: Webinar Replay | Space-Based Data Centers: Inside the Race to Move Compute to Orbit
summary: A webinar discussed the emerging trend of space-based data centers, focusing on the business case, technical challenges, and commercial viability of moving computing infrastructure to orbit. Industry players debated the feasibility and potential benefits of orbital compute stacks.

title: Keysight and Sateliot to Explore Blockchain Frameworks for 5G NTN Security
summary: Keysight Technologies is leading an ESA program to explore blockchain frameworks for detecting anomalies in 5G non-terrestrial networks (NTN) security. The collaboration involves Sateliot as a partner in this initiative.

title: Viasat Demonstrates Automotive Voice Call in BMW Platform
summary: Viasat showcased a satellite voice call integrated into a BMW vehicle's infotainment system during a 5G Automotive Association event. This demonstration highlights advancements in automotive connectivity using satellite technology.

title: AST SpaceMobile Discloses Delays to Launch Campaign, Explores Vertical Integration
summary: AST SpaceMobile has delayed its launch campaign to early 2027 and is exploring vertical integration. The company also initiated a share sale to raise additional funds.

title: Swissto12 Secures $70M in Series C Round
summary: Swissto12 has raised $70 million in a Series C funding round to expand its manufacturing capabilities. The company reports a strong growth trajectory with increasing revenues.
